text:
The pydantic mypy plugin ran into unexpected behavior [pydantic-unexpected]

### Initial Checks

- [x] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

I know I shouldn't have parent_attr as a property for a child class (mypy will complain about it), but I got a bug from the pydantic plugin:

uv run mypy main.py --strict
main.py:8: error: The pydantic mypy plugin ran into unexpected behavior: sym_node.node: Decorator:11(
  Var(parent_attr)
  NameExpr(computed_field [pydantic.fields.computed_field])
  FuncDef:13(
    parent_attr
    Args(
      Var(self))
    def (self: Any) -> str?
    Property
    Block:14(
      ReturnStmt:14(
        MemberExpr:14(
          NameExpr(self)
          child_attr))))) (of type <class 'mypy.nodes.Decorator'>)
Please consider reporting this bug at https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/new/choose so we can try to fix it!  [pydantic-unexpected]
main.py:11: error: Decorators on top of @property are not supported  [prop-decorator]
main.py:11: err
label: bug

text:
model_validator mode='wrap' receives raw dict instead of partially validated model

BUG REPORT: openai.BadRequestError when using Gemini models for tool calls
============================================================

## Error Message
openai.BadRequestError: Error code: 400 - {'error': {'message': 'Tool call function arguments must be valid JSON.', 'type': 'invalid_request_error'}}

## Code to Reproduce
```python
from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent
from autogen_ext.models.openai import OpenAIChatCompletionClient

model = OpenAIChatCompletionClient(
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    api_key=os.environ["GEMINI_API_KEY"],
)
agent = AssistantAgent("helper", model_client=model, tools=[my_tool])
result = await agent.run(task="What is the weather in NYC?")
```

## Environment
autogen-agentchat==0.5.6
autogen-ext==0.5.6
openai==1.82.0
Python 3.11.5

## Expected Behavior
Tool call should execute successfully with Gemini models

## Actual Behavior
Raises openai.BadRequestError
label: bug

text:
2.12.0: None: None throws an error

### Initial Checks

- [x] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

After upgrading `pydantic` to v2.12.0 and using python 3.14.0-rc2 (from nixpkgs), `supabase-auth` started raising an error for typing a field of a pydantic class as `field : None = None`, where it previously worked before. This was first noted by user @vite-falcon in https://github.com/supabase/supabase-py/pull/1252. I believe this is a bug because the linked explanation refers to a problem with field names and field types clashing, but this is a case of field name and *default value* clashing.

In order to reproduce, clone the repository, `uv lock --sync` to upgrade `pydantic` to 2.12 then drop into a shell with python 3.14 and run `import supabase_auth`, which throws the following error:

```
Python 3.14.0rc2 (main, Aug 14 2025, 12:19:47) [GCC 14.3.0] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import supabase_auth
Traceback (mos
label: bug

text:
Subclass Serialization Includes Child Fields with MISSING Sentinel

### Initial Checks

- [x] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

# Pydantic Bug Report: Subclass Serialization Includes Child Fields with MISSING Sentinel

## Summary

When a base class contains fields with `| MISSING` type annotation AND a container field uses `| MISSING` in its union type, Pydantic fails to properly serialize subclasses according to their declared parent type. Instead, it serializes the full runtime (child) type, including fields that should be excluded.

## Versions

- **pydantic**: 2.12.5
- **pydantic_core**: 2.41.5

## Minimal Reproducer

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, TypeAdapter
from pydantic.experimental.missing_sentinel import MISSING

class Base(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')
    base_field: str | MISSING = MISSING  # Key ingredient 1

class Parent(Base):
    parent_field: str

class Child(Parent):
    child_field: str  # Shoul
label: bug

text:
Mypy pluging not checking arg-type error on BaseModel inherited class

### Initial Checks

- [x] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

When I run mypy without the pydantic plugin, it correctly checks that there is an arg-type error:

uv run mypy main.py --strict
main.py:8: error: Argument "attr" to "Model" has incompatible type "str"; expected "float"  [arg-type]

but when I add it to pyproject.toml:

[tool.mypy]
plugins = ["pydantic.mypy"]

I get the following:

uv run mypy main.py --strict
Success: no issues found in 1 source file

As far as I tested, it happens for every type (not only when trying to assign a str to a float attribute) 

### Example Code

```Python
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Model(BaseModel):
    attr: float

def main() -> None:
    Model(attr="attr")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

### Python, Pydantic & OS Version

```Text
pydantic version: 2.13.3
        pydantic-core version: 2.46.3
          pydantic-core build: profile=re
label: bug

text:
2.12.0: field_serializer not called by model_dump(mode='json', serialize_as_any=True) in v2.12

### Initial Checks

- [x] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

Field serializers seem to be ignored in version 2.12 when calling `model_dump` with `serialize_as_any=True`.  The example code fails with a `PydanticSerializationError` on v2.12 but runs fine on 2.11.9.  Expected behavior is no error.  Serializer *is* called correctly when `serialize_as_any=False`.

### Example Code

```Python
from pydantic import BaseModel, field_serializer, SerializationInfo
import numpy as np

class Test(BaseModel, arbitrary_types_allowed=True):
    ar: np.ndarray
    
    @field_serializer("ar")
    def serialize_ar(self, v: np.ndarray, info: SerializationInfo):
        return v.tolist()

test = Test(ar=np.identity(4))
test.model_dump(mode='json', serialize_as_any=True)
```

### Python, Pydantic & OS Version

```Text
pydantic version: 2.12.0
        pydantic-core version: 2.41.1
          py
label: bug

text:
Base64 model export

### Initial Checks

- [x] I have searched Google & GitHub for similar requests and couldn't find anything
- [x] I have read and followed [the docs](https://docs.pydantic.dev) and still think this feature is missing

### Description

Hi,

Currently, pydantic supports exporting a dict with `model_dump` and a JSON-encoded string version of it with `model_dump_json`, as described in https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/serialization/#modelmodel_dump

I think it would be nice to also cover the export of the model to base64 / base64 string, could be named `model_dump_base64` /  `model_dump_json_base64`? Idea being that pydantic would take care of the bytes encoding / decoding, and base64 encoding. Less code on user side, and better performance!

### Affected Components

- [ ] [Compatibility between releases](https://docs.pydantic.dev/changelog/)
- [ ] [Data validation/parsing](https://docs.pydantic.dev/concepts/models/#basic-model-usage)
- [x] [Data serialization](h
label: feature

text:
Add `exclude_extra` option to `model_dump` and `model_dump_json`

### Initial Checks

- [x] I have searched Google & GitHub for similar requests and couldn't find anything
- [x] I have read and followed [the docs](https://docs.pydantic.dev) and still think this feature is missing

### Description

If a pydantic base model allows extra fields, they get included in `model_dump` by default, e.g.
```python
class A(BaseModel, extra="allow"):
    a: int

a = A(a=1, b="test")
print(a)  # A(a=1, b='test')
print(a.model_dump())  # {'a': 1, 'b': 'test'}
```
There are cases where we might want to exclude the extra fields from being dumped. The requested feature being something like
```python
print(a.model_dump(exclude_extra=True))  # {'a': 1}
```
`exclude_extra` would be `False` by default preserving the current behaviour.

Technically, this can be done today by doing something like
```python
a_dict = a.model_dump()
for k in a.model_extra:
    a_dict.pop(k)
```
But this is cumbersome, especially
label: feature

text:
Add Cadwyn test suite to pydantic

### Initial Checks

- [x] I have searched Google & GitHub for similar requests and couldn't find anything
- [x] I have read and followed [the docs](https://docs.pydantic.dev) and still think this feature is missing

### Description

(based on [this](https://docs.pydantic.dev/dev/contributing/#adding-your-library-as-part-of-pydantics-third-party-test-suite))

[Cadwyn](https://github.com/zmievsa/cadwyn) is an API versioning framework based on FastAPI and pydantic. It generates pydantic models and python enums on the fly based on migrations written by the end users of the framework.

For example, the following version change from Cadwyn will make cadwyn generate another version of UserCreateRequest and UserResource while renaming the field `addresses` to `address` and changing its type from `list[str]` to `str`.

```python
class ChangeAddressToList(VersionChange):
    description = (
        "Give user the ability to have multiple addresses at the same t
label: feature

text:
More extensive docs on what pydantic-core schema is, and how __get_pydantic_core_schema__ should be used

### Initial Checks

- [X] I have searched Google & GitHub for similar requests and couldn't find anything
- [X] I have read and followed [the docs](https://docs.pydantic.dev) and still think this feature is missing

### Description

As it stands, __get_pydantic_core_schema__ looks like a magic incantation. [The docs for customizing validation with __get_pydantic_core_schema__](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/types/#customizing-validation-with-__get_pydantic_core_schema__) tell us it's used to tell pydantic how to generate the pydantic-core schema, but the docs for what exactly it is, and how it factors into custom class creation, are missing.

It would be good to have documentation explaining exactly what `pydantic-core schema` is, what pydantic does with it, and how it can be manipulated to construct custom types.

### Affected Components

- [ ] [Compatibility between re
label: docs

text:
Inconsistent documentation of `field_serializer` method signature error

### Initial Checks

- [X] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

I'm trying to implement a custom field serializer for the first time. I have a model with an `Optional[set[str]]` field that I want to serialize as a list. My model raised an error during serialization. The error message included the text `For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.8/u/field-serializer-signature`.

That section of the docs ("Unrecognized `field_serializer` signature")  has a subsection with "Valid serializer signatures are:" but that list only includes _model_ serializer methods, not field serializer methods. The next section of the docs is "Unrecognized `model_serializer` signature",, but it has no examples section at all.

I wonder if the examples currently under the field serializer section  (again, they all seem to be examples of model serializers) should be moved under the next section, and new e
label: docs

text:
[Documentation] `docs/errors/usage_errors.md` incorrectly reference `model_serializer` in the `field-serializer-signature` section

### Initial Checks

- [X] I confirm that I'm using Pydantic V2

### Description

Hello Dear Pydantic maintainers. I hope you are well in this hot summer (if you're up north)

Something is bothering me with the documentation, I must say.

It seems to me that this section of the documentation https://docs.pydantic.dev/2.8/errors/usage_errors/#field-serializer-signature heavily mentions `model_serializer` while it should actually mention `field_serializer`.

If i interpret this section correctly, everything seems right *except* the term "model_serializer". 

Would a basic string replacement s/model_serializer/field_serializer/ be enough ?

Should I submit a patch in this way ?

Also, there's no such (valuable) information in the `model-serializer-signature` section.
Would you be interested in an another update to fill this blank ?

I have found a lot of docum
label: docs

text:
pydantic.validate_call makes a copy of the list in order to validate it?

This is new to me. Could someone help me understand why pydantic has to make a copy of the list in order to validate it? 

If so, does it mean `pydantic.validate_call` is not supposed to be used for any function that does in-place operation inside? Is the behavior mentioned somewhere in the doc?

Thanks,

_Originally posted by @xyshell in https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/8165#issuecomment-2381601386_
label: question

text:
How to customize fields？

### Initial Checks

- [x] I have searched Google & GitHub for similar requests and couldn't find anything
- [x] I have read and followed [the docs](https://docs.pydantic.dev) and still think this feature is missing

### Description

I want to add my own fields to an existing field with type annotations. How can I do this? Can I inherit FieldInfo and set new properties?

For example

```py
class CustomFiledInfo(FieldInfo):
    c_type: bool = None

class Demo(BaseModel):
    tags: str = CustomFiledInfo(c_type=False)
```

### Affected Components

- [ ] [Compatibility between releases](https://docs.pydantic.dev/changelog/)
- [ ] [Data validation/parsing](https://docs.pydantic.dev/concepts/models/#basic-model-usage)
- [ ] [Data serialization](https://docs.pydantic.dev/concepts/serialization/) - `.model_dump()` and `.model_dump_json()`
- [ ] [JSON Schema](https://docs.pydantic.dev/concepts/json_schema/)
- [ ] [Dataclasses](https://docs.pydantic.dev/concepts/dataclas
label: question
